item.title: KKR Plans to Take Japan’s Taiyo Private in $3.2 Billion Deal
item.description: KKR & Co. is planning a tender offer for Taiyo Holdings Co. in a transaction valuing the Japanese firm at about ¥500 billion ($3.2 billion).

item.title: India IPO Filings Defy Stock Downturn With Near-Record March
item.description: India notched its second-highest month ever in filings for initial public offerings, even as broader equity markets remained subdued and investor sentiment stayed cautious.

item.title: Sugar Trade Upended as War Chokes Flows From Major Refining Hub
item.description: Global sugar prices are rebounding after a prolonged slump, as the war in Iran squeezes supplies from a major refining hub, boosting sales from producers outside the Middle East.

item.title: Three Teens Facing Terrorism Charges Over BofA Bombing Attempt
item.description: French prosecutors are seeking terrorism charges against an adult and three teens aged 16-17 over a foiled bombing attack near the Bank of America Corp. office in Paris, seen as a likely retaliation to the war with Iran.

item.title: Tanker Carrying Iran Crude Signals India After 7-Year Import Gap
item.description: A US-sanctioned vessel carrying Iranian crude oil from deep inside the Persian Gulf is now indicating it’s bound for India, in what could be the first such import in nearly seven years.

item.title: Paschi’s Ousted CEO Lovaglio ‘Confident’ He’ll Get New Term
item.description: Ousted Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio said he expects to win a new term at the helm of the Italian lender despite efforts by the board of directors to replace him.

item.title: Amazon Fuels Record First Quarter for Company Bond Sales in EMEA
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has almost single-handedly spurred the best-ever opening quarter for corporate debt sales in Europe, even as markets face huge disruption from the war in Iran.

item.title: UK Factory PMI Shows Worst Supply Chain Stress Since 2022
item.description: UK factories are enduring the most intense strain on supply chains and steepest price pressures since the aftermath of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, according to a closely watched survey.

item.title: Indian Bonds Face Pressure as RBI’s Forex Curbs Stoke Rate Risk
item.description: Indian bonds may be heading into a challenging stretch after the Reserve Bank of India’s dramatic move to cap onshore currency wagers raises the possibility of stronger measures — including possible interest rate hikes — to support the rupee.

item.title: France and Japan Defense Chiefs Pledge Cooperation in Pacific
item.description: The defense ministers of France and Japan pledged to work together on security in the Pacific as the conflict in the Middle East draws away American military supplies and troops from the region.

item.title: India Says PE Investments Before April 2017 Won’t Face Extra Tax
item.description: Buyout firms sitting on billions of dollars of legacy assets in India got some relief this week, when the country’s finance ministry said it won’t apply anti-tax avoidance laws to investments made before April 1, 2017.

item.title: Japan March Duty-Free Sales Rebound as China Driven Slump Eases
item.description: Duty-free sales at Japan’s top department stores rose in March, signaling a tentative rebound after months of weakness tied to a drop in Chinese tourist spending.

item.title: Javier Blas on Why Oil Could Go Much, Much Higher
item.description: Understanding the global crisis.

item.title: German Growth Forecast Cut in Half on Iran War, Advisers Say
item.description: Germany’s economy will grow at less than half the pace expected just a few months ago due to the conflict in the Middle East, according to the country’s leading research institutes.

item.title: Odd Lots: Blas on Why Oil Could Go Much, Much Higher (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Blas on Why Oil Could Go Much, Much Higher (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Blas on Why Oil Could Go Much, Much Higher (Podcast)】

item.title: US Allows Kazakhstan to Continue Transit of Russian Oil to China
item.description: The US administration will allow Kazakhstan to continue transiting Russian pipeline crude to China until next March, according to the Central Asian nation’s energy ministry.

item.title: Mozambique Fully Repaid $701 Million IMF Debt Early, Bank Says
item.description: Mozambique repaid early and in full about $701 million it owed the International Monetary Fund, implying the cancellation of a mission to the southeast African nation targeted for August, according to Standard Bank Ltd.

item.title: Euphoria in Global Stocks Masks Underlying Angst Over Oil Prices
item.description: A worldwide stock surge is masking deeper anxiety over the global economic outlook that may cut the relief rally short.

item.title: US Bonds Gain as Iran Withdrawal Hopes Fuel Fed Rate-Cut Bets
item.description: US Treasuries climbed on speculation that an imminent end to the war in Iran may pave the way for the Federal Reserve to start cutting interest rates again.

item.title: Swiss Factory Index Jumps to Indicate First Growth Since 2022
item.description: A gauge of Swiss manufacturing activity unexpected surged in March to indicate expansion for the first time in more than three years.

item.title: Gilts and European Bonds Surge as Oil Drops on Iran War Optimism
item.description: UK and European government bonds surged, sending yields tumbling, as oil prices slumped on hopes that the Iran war may end in the coming few weeks.

item.title: Nigeria Weighs $5 Billion Swap Deal With UAE Bank as Costs Rise
item.description: Nigeria plans to raise $5 billion from the United Arab Emirates’ largest lender in a derivatives deal to cut borrowing costs, joining a growing number of African nations using the instruments as the war in Iran drives yields higher.

item.title: India Begins Counting Over 1 Billion People in Digital Census
item.description: India has begun its long-delayed national census, a policy-shaping exercise that will collect a wide range of data and, for the first time since independence, include citizens’ caste.

item.title: Oil Slides Below $100 on Optimism Over Iran War Resolution
item.description: Oil tumbled after President Donald Trump again signaled a potential end to the Iran war, even as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed and more US troops head to the region.

item.title: Asian Stocks Rally Most in a Year on Hopes Iran War May End
item.description: Asian stocks rallied the most in nearly a year, tracking Wall Street’s rally on optimism that the war in Iran may end in the near future.

item.title: Australia Spending Holds Up as War Drives Oil Spike, CBA Says
item.description: Australian consumer spending has held up in the face of the Middle East conflict and the resulting surge in oil prices, the nation’s top lender Commonwealth Bank of Australia said.

item.title: Coca-Cola Plans $1 Billion South Africa Investment Through 2030
item.description: Coca-Cola Co. and its two authorized bottling partners in South Africa plan to invest 17.6 billion rand ($1 billion) in domestic operations through 2030 to expand capacity and bolster distribution.

item.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise on Iran War Optimism
European Stock Futures Jump on Optimism For Iran War Conclusion
item.description: European stock futures jumped on Wednesday morning, tracking a rally on Wall Street after President Donald Trump said he foresaw ending the war on Iran within two to three weeks.

item.title: Indian Rupee Set for More Chaos as Banks Unwind $30 Billion in Arbitrage Trades
item.description: The biggest shock to India’s currency market in years is set to worsen as banks prepare to unwind billions of dollars more in arbitrage trades.
